name: Ghawar Field
note: World's largest conventional oilfield; backbone of Saudi production for 70+ years

name: Safaniya Field
note: World's largest offshore oilfield; heavy crude supply to Asian refiners

name: Greater Burgan Field
note: World's second-largest oilfield; primary source of Kuwait export revenue

name: Rumaila Field
note: Iraq's largest field; key recovery project post-2003 driving OPEC quota debates

name: Permian Basin - Midland Sub-basin
note: Tight-oil epicenter; Pioneer/ExxonMobil anchor; US shale price-setter

name: Permian Basin - Delaware Sub-basin
note: Fastest-growing US sub-basin; Chevron/Occidental/Devon major operators

name: Eagle Ford Shale
note: Light sweet crude; condensate-rich windows; key US export grade

name: Bakken Formation
note: Shale pioneer; Whiting/Hess/Continental; Williston Basin WTI-linked pricing

name: Vaca Muerta Shale
note: World's second-largest shale oil reserve; key LatAm unconventional growth driver

name: Kashagan Field
note: Complex sour crude; H2S challenges; critical for Caspian export routes CPC

name: Tengiz Field
note: Giant sour oil field; major expansion project (FGP) ramping capacity

name: Samotlor Field
note: Once world's largest field; mature decline but still significant in Russia's mix

name: Priobskoye Field
note: Russia's top producing single field; tight reservoir requiring EOR investment

name: Johan Sverdrup Field
note: Europe's largest discovery in decades; low-carbon intensity crude; full production 2022

name: Bonny / Niger Delta Complex
note: Nigeria's primary export blend; theft and sabotage chronically constrain output

name: Stabroek Block
note: Fastest-growing new oil province globally; 11+ Gb resource; Liza Phase 2 ramping

name: Cantarell Complex
note: Former world giant now in severe decline; heavy Maya crude; nitrogen injection halted collapse

name: Kirkuk Field
note: Supergiant disputed between Baghdad and Kurdistan; export via Turkey pipeline

name: Ahvaz Field
note: Iran's largest field; under US sanctions constraining global market access

name: North Field (South Pars)
note: World's single largest natural gas reservoir shared with Iran (South Pars); backbone of LNG trade

name: Ras Laffan LNG Terminal
note: World's largest LNG export complex; feeds Asian and European term contracts

name: Marcellus/Utica Appalachian Basin
note: World's most productive shale gas basin; sets US Henry Hub price floor

name: Bovanenkovo Field
note: Russia's largest producing gas field; feeds Power of Siberia and European pipelines

name: Sabetta / Yamal LNG Terminal
note: Arctic LNG hub; ice-class tankers supply Asia year-round; sanctions constraining expansion

name: NW Shelf + Gorgon LNG
note: Australia's LNG cornerstone; long-term contracts to Japan/Korea/China underpin Asian pricing

name: Groningen Gas Field
note: Once Europe's largest gas field; seismic damage forced production shutdown by 2024

name: Sabine Pass LNG Terminal
note: First US Lower-48 LNG export terminal; price-setter for US LNG global market

name: Corpus Christi LNG Terminal
note: Major US export hub; Stage 3 expansion adding significant capacity 2025-2026

name: Haynesville Shale
note: Deep, dry gas play; preferred feed gas basin for Gulf Coast LNG exports

name: Prelude FLNG
note: World's largest floating LNG vessel; remote Browse Basin development

name: McArthur River Mine
note: World's highest-grade uranium mine; key to global U supply security

name: Cigar Lake Mine
note: World's largest high-grade uranium mine; jet-boring method in soft ore

name: Inkai In-Situ Leach Mine
note: Kazakhstan ISL operation; Kazatomprom controls ~23% of global output

name: Tortkuduk & Mynkuduk ISL
note: Largest Orano operation globally; underpins French nuclear fuel supply

name: Husab Uranium Mine
note: Second-largest uranium mine globally; Chinese-owned; key to Beijing's nuclear expansion

name: Rossing Uranium Mine
note: One of world's oldest uranium mines (1976); open pit in low-grade alaskite

name: Olympic Dam Mine
note: Unique IOCG deposit; also copper/gold/silver co-products; export licence politics matter

name: Powder River Basin
note: US largest coal region; low-sulfur sub-bituminous; thermal coal for US power

name: Bowen Basin / Hunter Valley
note: World's top metallurgical coal export region; Queensland coking coal sets Asia benchmark

name: Shanxi Coal Basin
note: China's coal heartland; domestic supply anchor; reserve estimates underpin global thermal pricing

name: Kuzbass (Kuznetsk Basin)
note: Russia's largest coal basin; thermal and coking; export to Asia via Siberian rail

name: Mahanadi Coalfields
note: India's highest-output coalfield; critical for power security in world's 3rd largest coal consumer

name: Carlin Trend
note: World's second richest gold district; disseminated Carlin-type deposits; JV formed 2019

name: Muruntau Gold Mine
note: World's largest single open-pit gold mine by output; fully state-owned; opaque reporting

name: Olimpiada Mine
note: Russia's largest gold mine and Polyus flagship; refractory ore processed via bio-oxidation

name: Kalgoorlie Super Pit (KCGM)
note: Australia's most iconic gold mine; 600m deep open pit; Golden Mile ore system

name: Grasberg Mine (gold)
note: World's largest gold mine by reserve; also massive copper producer; high altitude underground transition

name: Pueblo Viejo Mine
note: Caribbean's largest gold mine; major expansion approved 2023; high-grade epithermal

name: Boddington Gold Mine
note: Australia's largest gold producer; open pit porphyry; also significant copper credit

name: Penasquito Mine
note: World's largest silver mine; polymetallic (gold/zinc/lead credits); open pit skarn

name: Fresnillo Mine
note: World's top primary silver producer historically; underground epithermal veins since colonial era

name: Cannington Mine (silver)
note: Australia's largest silver-lead-zinc mine; one of world's highest-grade underground silver ops

name: Dukat Mine
note: Russia's largest silver mine; epithermal vein; sanctions redirect offtake to Asian buyers

name: Mogalakwena Platinum Mine
note: World's largest open-pit platinum mine; Platreef deposit on northern limb of Bushveld

name: Impala Rustenburg Operations
note: Largest underground PGM operation globally; Merensky and UG2 reef mining

name: Norilsk Nickel Talnakh Operations
note: World's dominant palladium producer (40%+ global supply); nickel-copper-PGM sulphide ore; sanctions risk key

name: Stillwater Mine
note: Only significant US PGM producer; J-M Reef deposit; critical US supply chain asset

name: Zimplats - Ngezi Operations
note: Great Dyke hosts world's second-largest PGM resource; Zimbabwe indigenisation risk factor

name: South Deep Mine
note: World's deepest gold mine (>3.9 km); mechanised; Witwatersrand conglomerate reef

name: Lihir Gold Mine
note: Active volcanic island open pit; geothermal dewatering challenge; major Pacific gold source

name: Turquoise Ridge / Twin Creeks
note: High-grade underground Carlin-type; part of NGM JV; among highest-margin US gold operations

name: San Cristobal Mine
note: Bolivia's largest mine; zinc-lead-silver; state pressure on royalties ongoing

name: Ordos Basin Coal Field
note: The Ordos Basin in Inner Mongolia is China's largest coal-producing region, anchored by giant open-pit and underground mines.

name: Shendong Coalfield
note: Shendong is one of the world's largest coal mining complexes, straddling the Shaanxi and Inner Mongolia border.

name: Haerwusu Open-Pit Mine
note: Haerwusu is among the largest open-pit coal mines in China, located in the Junggar coalfield of Inner Mongolia.

name: Pingshuo Open-Pit Mine
note: Pingshuo is a large surface coal mining complex near Shuozhou in northern Shanxi province.

name: Yulin (Northern Shaanxi) Coal Base
note: The Yulin coal base in northern Shaanxi is one of China's principal thermal coal supply regions.

name: Jharia Coalfield
note: Jharia in Jharkhand is India's principal source of prime coking coal and a major bituminous coalfield.

name: Singrauli Coalfield
note: Singrauli is a major thermal coalfield feeding a cluster of large power stations in central India.

name: Korba Coalfield
note: Korba in Chhattisgarh is one of India's largest thermal coal mining areas with multiple open-cast mines.

name: Talcher Coalfield
note: Talcher in Odisha holds India's largest coal reserves and is a leading open-cast thermal coal producer.

name: Gevra Open-Cast Mine
note: Gevra is the largest single open-cast coal mine in India, located in the Korba district of Chhattisgarh.

name: East Kalimantan Coal Belt
note: East Kalimantan hosts Indonesia's largest thermal coal mines and is a leading global seaborne coal supply region.

name: Kaltim Prima Coal (Sangatta)
note: Kaltim Prima Coal at Sangatta is one of the world's largest single open-pit thermal coal mines.

name: Adaro Tutupan Mine
note: Adaro's Tutupan mine in the Tabalong district is one of Indonesia's largest single-pit coal operations.

name: South Sumatra Coal Belt
note: South Sumatra holds large low-rank coal reserves mined principally by Bukit Asam around Tanjung Enim.

name: Peak Downs Mine
note: Peak Downs in the Bowen Basin is one of the world's largest metallurgical coal mines.

name: Curragh Coal Mine
note: Curragh near Blackwater is a major Bowen Basin metallurgical and thermal coal mine.

name: Mount Arthur Coal Mine
note: Mount Arthur near Muswellbrook is one of the largest thermal coal mines in the Hunter Valley.

name: Carmichael Coal Mine
note: Carmichael in the Galilee Basin is a large thermal coal mine exporting via the Adani Abbot Point terminal.

name: Central Appalachian Coal Basin
note: Central Appalachia is the principal US source of high-quality metallurgical and thermal coal.

name: Illinois Basin Coal Field
note: The Illinois Basin is a major US thermal coal region spanning Illinois, Indiana and western Kentucky.

name: North Antelope Rochelle Mine
note: North Antelope Rochelle in the Powder River Basin is the largest coal mine in the United States.

name: Elga Coal Complex
note: Elga in Yakutia is one of Russia's largest coking coal deposits, served by a dedicated rail line to the Pacific.

name: Raspadskaya Coal Mine
note: Raspadskaya near Mezhdurechensk is one of Russia's largest coking coal mines in the Kuzbass.

name: Kansk-Achinsk Lignite Basin
note: The Kansk-Achinsk basin in Siberia holds vast brown-coal reserves mined for domestic power generation.

name: Witbank (Emalahleni) Coalfield
note: The Witbank coalfield in Mpumalanga is South Africa's principal thermal coal mining region.

name: Grootegeluk Coal Mine
note: Grootegeluk in the Waterberg coalfield supplies the Matimba and Medupi power stations.

name: Cerrejon Coal Mine
note: Cerrejon in La Guajira is one of the world's largest open-pit export thermal coal mines.

name: Calenturitas / La Jagua Mines
note: The Cesar coal district hosts large open-pit thermal coal mines exporting through Caribbean ports.

name: Upper Silesian Coal Basin
note: The Upper Silesian Coal Basin is the largest hard coal mining area in the European Union.

name: Rhenish Lignite Mining Area
note: The Rhenish district including the Hambach and Garzweiler pits is the largest lignite mining area in Europe.

name: Bogatyr Coal Mine (Ekibastuz)
note: Bogatyr at Ekibastuz is one of the world's largest open-pit coal mines, supplying Kazakh and Russian power plants.

name: Tavan Tolgoi Coal Deposit
note: Tavan Tolgoi in the South Gobi is one of the world's largest untapped coking coal deposits, exported to China.

name: Elk Valley Coal Operations
note: The Elk Valley mines in southeastern British Columbia are Canada's principal source of seaborne metallurgical coal.

name: Qinhuangdao Coal Port
note: Qinhuangdao is the world's largest coal handling port, shipping coal from northern China to the south.

name: Port of Newcastle (Coal)
note: Newcastle in New South Wales is the world's largest coal export port, serving Hunter Valley thermal coal.

name: Dalrymple Bay Coal Terminal
note: Dalrymple Bay near Mackay is a major export terminal for Bowen Basin metallurgical coal.

name: Puerto Bolivar Coal Terminal
note: Puerto Bolivar is a deep-water coal export terminal serving the Cerrejon mine in northern Colombia.

name: Richards Bay Coal Terminal
note: Richards Bay is the principal export gateway for South African thermal coal to Asia and Europe.

name: Budenovskoye Uranium Mine
note: Budenovskoye in southern Kazakhstan is one of the world's largest in-situ recovery uranium deposits.

name: Karatau ISL Uranium Mine
note: Karatau is a large in-situ leach uranium operation in the Chu-Sarysu basin of southern Kazakhstan.

name: Akdala ISL Uranium Mine
note: Akdala in the Shu-Sarysu province was one of the first in-situ recovery uranium mines in Kazakhstan.

name: Kharasan ISL Uranium Mine
note: Kharasan in the Syr Darya basin is a major in-situ recovery uranium operation in Kazakhstan.

name: Central Mynkuduk ISL Mine
note: Central Mynkuduk in the Chu-Sarysu basin is an in-situ recovery uranium mine operated by Kazatomprom subsidiary Ortalyk.

name: Irkol ISL Uranium Mine
note: Irkol is an in-situ recovery uranium mine in the Syr Darya uranium province of southern Kazakhstan.

name: Key Lake Mill
note: Key Lake in the Athabasca Basin is the world's largest uranium mill, processing high-grade McArthur River ore.

name: Rabbit Lake Uranium Operation
note: Rabbit Lake in the Athabasca Basin is one of the longest-operating uranium mining and milling sites in Canada.

name: McClean Lake Mill
note: McClean Lake mill in the Athabasca Basin processes high-grade ore slurry from the Cigar Lake mine.

name: Langer Heinrich Uranium Mine
note: Langer Heinrich is a surface uranium mine in the Namib Desert that restarted production in 2024.

name: Arlit (SOMAIR) Uranium Mine
note: The SOMAIR open-pit operation near Arlit has been a mainstay of Niger's uranium production for decades.

name: Akouta (COMINAK) Uranium Mine
note: The COMINAK underground mine near Akokan was one of the world's largest underground uranium mines before closing in 2021.

name: Navoi Uranium Mining (Central Kyzylkum)
note: Uzbekistan's uranium is produced by in-situ leach across the Central Kyzylkum desert, centred on Navoi.

name: Priargunsky Uranium Mine
note: Priargunsky near Krasnokamensk is Russia's largest uranium mining and milling complex.

name: Khiagda ISL Uranium Mine
note: Khiagda in Buryatia is an in-situ leach uranium operation exploiting a cluster of Vitim basin deposits.

name: Dalur ISL Uranium Mine
note: Dalur in the Kurgan region is an in-situ recovery uranium mine in the Trans-Urals province.

name: Four Mile Uranium Mine
note: Four Mile near Beverley in South Australia is an in-situ recovery uranium operation in the Frome Basin.

name: Honeymoon Uranium Mine
note: Honeymoon in South Australia is an in-situ recovery uranium mine that restarted production in 2024.

name: Yili Basin Uranium Mines
note: The Yili Basin in Xinjiang hosts China's principal in-situ leach uranium operations.

name: White Mesa Uranium Mill
note: White Mesa near Blanding is the only conventional uranium mill operating in the United States.

name: Goldstrike (Betze-Post) Mine
note: Goldstrike is the flagship open-pit and underground operation of the Barrick-Newmont Nevada Gold Mines joint venture on the Carlin Trend.

name: Cortez Mine
note: Cortez is a large gold operation on the Battle Mountain-Eureka trend producing close to one million ounces annually.

name: Cripple Creek & Victor Mine
note: Cripple Creek & Victor is Colorado's largest gold mine, a heap-leach open pit in a historic mining district.

name: Fort Knox Mine
note: Fort Knox is a large open-pit heap-leach gold mine near Fairbanks operated by Kinross.

name: Tropicana Gold Mine
note: Tropicana is a large open-pit gold mine on the edge of the Great Victoria Desert in Western Australia.

name: St Ives Gold Mine
note: St Ives near Kambalda is a combined open-pit and underground gold operation in the Eastern Goldfields.

name: Granny Smith Mine
note: Granny Smith is an underground gold mine near Laverton in the Western Australian goldfields.

name: Jundee Gold Mine
note: Jundee is an underground gold mine in the Yandal belt of Western Australia operated by Northern Star.

name: Sunrise Dam Gold Mine
note: Sunrise Dam is a deep open-pit and underground gold mine near Laverton in Western Australia.

name: Telfer Gold Mine
note: Telfer is a large gold-copper mine in the Paterson Province of Western Australia's remote Great Sandy Desert.

name: Natalka Gold Mine
note: Natalka is a large open-pit gold mine in the Magadan region of Russia's Far East operated by Polyus.

name: Blagodatnoye Mine
note: Blagodatnoye is an open-pit gold mine in the Krasnoyarsk region adjacent to Polyus' Olimpiada operation.

name: Kupol Gold Mine
note: Kupol is a high-grade underground gold-silver mine in the remote Chukotka region of northeastern Russia.

name: Verninskoye Mine
note: Verninskoye is a gold mine in the Bodaibo district of the Irkutsk region in eastern Siberia.

name: Kibali Gold Mine
note: Kibali in northeastern DRC is one of Africa's largest gold mines, with combined open-pit and underground operations.

name: Loulo-Gounkoto Complex
note: Loulo-Gounkoto in western Mali is a major underground and open-pit gold complex operated by Barrick.

name: Sadiola Gold Mine
note: Sadiola is a large open-pit gold mine in the Kayes region of western Mali near the Senegal border.

name: Fekola Gold Mine
note: Fekola is a large open-pit gold mine in southwestern Mali operated by B2Gold near the Senegal border.

name: Tarkwa Gold Mine
note: Tarkwa is one of the largest open-pit gold mines in Africa, located in southwestern Ghana.

name: Obuasi Gold Mine
note: Obuasi is a historic high-grade underground gold mine in Ghana's Ashanti region, redeveloped by AngloGold Ashanti.

name: Ahafo Gold Mine
note: Ahafo is a major open-pit and underground gold mine in central Ghana operated by Newmont.

name: Akyem Gold Mine
note: Akyem is an open-pit gold mine in eastern Ghana producing several hundred thousand ounces annually.

name: Essakane Gold Mine
note: Essakane is a large open-pit gold mine in northeastern Burkina Faso near the border with Niger and Mali.

name: Houndé Gold Mine
note: Houndé is an open-pit gold mine in southwestern Burkina Faso operated by Endeavour Mining.

name: Sukari Gold Mine
note: Sukari is Egypt's first and largest modern gold mine, located in the Eastern Desert near the Red Sea.

name: Geita Gold Mine
note: Geita is one of Tanzania's largest gold mines, located in the Lake Victoria goldfields.

name: Bulyanhulu Gold Mine
note: Bulyanhulu is a deep high-grade underground gold mine in the Lake Victoria goldfields of Tanzania.

name: North Mara Gold Mine
note: North Mara is a combined open-pit and underground gold mine in northern Tanzania near Lake Victoria.

name: Yanacocha Mine
note: Yanacocha in northern Peru is one of the largest gold mines in South America, a high-altitude open-pit operation.

name: Lagunas Norte Mine
note: Lagunas Norte is a high-altitude open-pit gold mine in the La Libertad region of northern Peru.

name: Merian Gold Mine
note: Merian is an open-pit gold mine in eastern Suriname operated by Newmont in the Guiana Shield.

name: Rosebel Gold Mine
note: Rosebel is an open-pit gold mine in northern Suriname operated by Zijin Mining.

name: Aurora Gold Mine
note: Aurora is an open-pit and underground gold mine in the Guiana Shield of northwestern Guyana.

name: Peñasquito Gold-Silver Mine
note: Peñasquito in Zacatecas is Mexico's largest open-pit mine, a major producer of gold, silver, lead and zinc.

name: Los Filos Gold Mine
note: Los Filos is an open-pit and underground gold mine in the Guerrero Gold Belt of southern Mexico.

name: Detour Lake Mine
note: Detour Lake in northeastern Ontario is one of Canada's largest gold mines, a high-tonnage open-pit operation.

name: Canadian Malartic Mine
note: Canadian Malartic in Quebec's Abitibi belt is one of the largest gold mines in Canada, transitioning to underground.

name: Meliadine Gold Mine
note: Meliadine is an underground and open-pit gold mine in Nunavut near Rankin Inlet in Arctic Canada.

name: Meadowbank (Amaruq) Complex
note: The Meadowbank complex with the Amaruq satellite is an open-pit gold operation in Nunavut, Arctic Canada.

name: Macassa Mine
note: Macassa is a deep high-grade underground gold mine at Kirkland Lake in Ontario's Abitibi greenstone belt.

name: Éléonore Gold Mine
note: Éléonore is an underground gold mine in the James Bay region of northern Quebec.

name: Porgera Gold Mine
note: Porgera is a high-grade gold mine in the highlands of Papua New Guinea operated as a joint venture by Barrick.

name: Martabe Gold Mine
note: Martabe is an open-pit gold-silver mine in North Sumatra, Indonesia.

name: Tujuh Bukit (Tumpangpitu) Mine
note: Tujuh Bukit is an open-pit gold-silver heap-leach mine in East Java, Indonesia.

name: Zarafshan-Newmont (Marjanbulak/Kokpatas)
note: The Zarafshan operations near Navoi process refractory gold ores from the Kokpatas and Daugyztau deposits.

name: Amantaytau Goldfields
note: Amantaytau is a gold mining operation in the Kyzylkum desert near Navoi in central Uzbekistan.

name: Kumtor Gold Mine
note: Kumtor is a high-altitude open-pit gold mine in the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan.

name: Zijinshan Gold-Copper Mine
note: Zijinshan in Fujian province is the flagship gold-copper mine of Zijin Mining and one of China's largest.

name: Jiaojia Gold Mine (Jiaodong Belt)
note: The Jiaojia mine in Shandong's Jiaodong gold belt is one of China's richest underground gold deposits.

name: Sanshandao Gold Mine
note: Sanshandao is a coastal underground gold mine in Shandong's Jiaodong peninsula with offshore reserves.

name: Didipio Gold-Copper Mine
note: Didipio is an underground gold-copper mine in the Philippines operated by OceanaGold.

name: Mansourah-Massarah Gold Mine
note: Mansourah-Massarah is Saudi Arabia's largest gold mine, an open-pit operation on the Arabian Shield.

name: Salares Norte Gold Mine
note: Salares Norte is a high-altitude open-pit gold-silver mine in the Atacama region of northern Chile.

name: Veladero Gold Mine
note: Veladero is a high-altitude open-pit heap-leach gold mine in the Andes of San Juan province, Argentina.

name: Cerro Negro Gold Mine
note: Cerro Negro is an underground gold-silver mine in the Deseado Massif of Santa Cruz province in Patagonia.

name: Fruta del Norte Mine
note: Fruta del Norte is a high-grade underground gold mine in the Amazonian foothills of southeastern Ecuador.

name: Ity Gold Mine
note: Ity is a large open-pit heap-leach gold mine in western Cote d'Ivoire operated by Endeavour Mining.

name: Sabodala-Massawa Gold Mine
note: Sabodala-Massawa is Senegal's largest gold mine, an open-pit operation in the southeastern Kedougou region.

name: Saucito Mine
note: Saucito is one of Mexico's largest primary silver mines, operated by Fresnillo in Zacatecas state.

name: Juanicipio Mine
note: Juanicipio is a high-grade silver mine in Zacatecas jointly owned by Fresnillo and MAG Silver.

name: San Julian Mine
note: San Julian is a silver-gold mine operated by Fresnillo on the Chihuahua-Durango border.

name: Las Chispas Mine
note: Las Chispas is a high-grade underground silver-gold mine in Sonora operated by SilverCrest Metals.

name: Palmarejo Mine
note: Palmarejo is a silver-gold mining complex in Chihuahua operated by Coeur Mining.

name: La Colorada Mine
note: La Colorada is an underground silver mine in Zacatecas operated by Pan American Silver.

name: San Dimas Mine
note: San Dimas is a silver-gold mine on the Durango-Sinaloa border operated by First Majestic Silver.

name: Guanajuato Mining District
note: The historic Guanajuato district hosts several active silver-gold mines along the Veta Madre vein system.

name: Uchucchacua Mine
note: Uchucchacua is a major primary silver mine in Peru's Lima region operated by Buenaventura.

name: Antamina Silver Byproduct
note: The Antamina copper-zinc mine in Ancash is among the world's largest silver byproduct producers.

name: Arcata Mine
note: Arcata is a high-grade silver-gold vein mine in Arequipa operated by Hochschild Mining.

name: Inmaculada Mine
note: Inmaculada is a silver-gold underground mine in Ayacucho operated by Hochschild Mining.

name: Yauliyacu Mine
note: Yauliyacu is a polymetallic silver mine in Peru's central Andes operated by Glencore.

name: Greens Creek Mine
note: Greens Creek on Alaska's Admiralty Island is one of the largest primary silver mines in the United States.

name: Lucky Friday Mine
note: Lucky Friday is a deep silver-lead-zinc mine in Idaho's Coeur d'Alene district operated by Hecla Mining.

name: Polkowice-Sieroszowice Mine (KGHM)
note: Polkowice-Sieroszowice is a KGHM copper-silver mine in the Legnica-Glogow copper district of Poland.

name: Ying Silver District
note: The Ying mining district in Henan is a high-grade silver-lead-zinc operation run by Silvercorp Metals.

name: Shuikoushan Lead-Zinc-Silver Mine
note: Shuikoushan in Hunan is a major Chinese polymetallic mine producing byproduct silver from lead-zinc ores.

name: Escobal Mine
note: Escobal in Guatemala is one of the world's largest silver deposits, currently suspended pending consultation.

name: Pulacayo District
note: The Pulacayo district near Potosi is a historic Bolivian silver-zinc area with ongoing small-scale production.

name: Cerro Rico de Potosi
note: Cerro Rico de Potosi is the historic Bolivian silver mountain still worked by mining cooperatives today.

name: Manantial Espejo Mine
note: Manantial Espejo is a silver-gold mine in Santa Cruz province of Patagonia operated by Pan American Silver.

name: Keno Hill Silver District
note: Keno Hill in the Yukon is a high-grade silver district operated by Hecla Mining.

name: Imiter Silver Mine
note: Imiter is Morocco's primary silver mine in the Anti-Atlas operated by Managem's SMI subsidiary.

name: Townsville Refinery (silver-lead)
note: Townsville hosts a lead refinery processing silver-rich concentrate from Mount Isa and Cannington in Queensland.

name: Kapan Mine
note: Kapan is a polymetallic gold-silver mine in southern Armenia operated by Chaarat Gold.

name: Waterval Smelter (Amplats Rustenburg)
note: The Rustenburg PGM operations on the western Bushveld are a leading source of platinum, now owned by Sibanye-Stillwater.

name: Amandelbult Complex
note: Amandelbult on the northwestern Bushveld is a major Anglo American Platinum mining complex.

name: Marikana Operations
note: Marikana on the western Bushveld is a large platinum mining complex operated by Sibanye-Stillwater.

name: Kroondal Mine
note: Kroondal is a shallow UG2-reef platinum mine near Rustenburg operated by Sibanye-Stillwater.

name: Two Rivers Mine
note: Two Rivers on the eastern Bushveld is a platinum joint venture between Implats and African Rainbow Minerals.

name: Modikwa Mine
note: Modikwa is an eastern Bushveld platinum mine jointly owned by Anglo American Platinum and ARM.

name: Mototolo Mine
note: Mototolo on the eastern Bushveld is a platinum mine operated by Anglo American Platinum.

name: Bafokeng-Rasimone Mine
note: Bafokeng-Rasimone is a western Bushveld platinum mine within the Implats group.

name: Unki Mine
note: Unki on Zimbabwe's Great Dyke is a platinum mine operated by Anglo American Platinum.

name: Mimosa Mine
note: Mimosa on the southern Great Dyke is a platinum joint venture between Implats and Sibanye-Stillwater.

name: East Boulder Mine
note: East Boulder mines the J-M Reef of Montana's Stillwater Complex, the only primary PGM source in the US.

name: Lac des Iles Mine
note: Lac des Iles in Ontario is one of the world's few primary palladium mines, operated by Impala Canada.

name: Mogalakwena Platreef (palladium)
note: The Mogalakwena Platreef ore in Limpopo carries a high palladium-to-platinum ratio, making it a top Pd source.

name: Platreef Project (Ivanhoe)
note: The Platreef project near Mokopane is a large palladium-platinum-nickel deposit being developed by Ivanhoe Mines.

name: Nadezhda Metallurgical Plant
note: The Nadezhda smelter at Norilsk processes Talnakh ores into the world's largest single source of refined palladium.

name: Kola MMC Monchegorsk Refinery
note: Nornickel's Kola Division at Monchegorsk refines nickel-copper-PGM matte into palladium and platinum products.

name: Booysendal Mine
note: Booysendal on the eastern Bushveld is a mechanised platinum mine operated by Northam Platinum.

name: Zondereinde Mine (Northam)
note: Zondereinde near Thabazimbi is the world's deepest platinum mine, operated by Northam Platinum.

name: Der Brochen Mine
note: Der Brochen on the eastern Bushveld is a platinum mine within the Anglo American Platinum portfolio.

name: Crocodile River Mine (Maroelabult)
note: Crocodile River on the western Bushveld is a platinum mine operated by Eastern Platinum.

name: Rio Tinto Kennecott PGM Byproduct
note: Rio Tinto's Kennecott copper refinery near Salt Lake City recovers minor platinum and palladium as byproducts.
